questions.question: A sentence has been given in Direct/Indirect speech. Out of the five alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Direct/Indirect speech. Rohini said, "I wrote a book".
options: Rohini said that she wrote a book.
Rohini said that she has written a book.
Rohini said that she had written a book.
Rohini said that she was writing a book.
questions.explanation: 'Wrote' (past tense) in direct speech should be converted to 'had written' (past perfect) in indirect speech. So only c) is correct.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, four sentences are given, which when properly arranged, make up a paragraph. Rearrange the given sentences and choose the option that provides the correct final order. P. Tourists, beggars, migrants, men, women - anyone can be mistaken for a child lifter by a mob primed for a killing and looking around for a victim. Q. This is over and above the lynchings in the name of the cow. R. At least 31 people have been killed over the last one year in 10 different states by lynch mobs mobilised by rumours of child lifting spread over WhatsApp. S. It is time to take concerted action against those who spread killer rumours, by the Centre and the states working together.
questions.explanation: RQ is a pair (R talks about death from a certain kind of lynching while Q talks about another kind (joined by 'This is over and above..')). S proposes a solution to the problem; hence, it has to be the concluding statement. Hence, (c) is the right answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, four sentences are given, which when properly arranged, make up a paragraph. Rearrange the given sentences and choose the option that provides the correct final order. P. Gamification is useful in making the employee transitioning process, that is otherwise quite humdrum, more interesting. Q. Organisations can help in this by devising policies and compliances familiar to the employee through various online games and quizzes. R. At last, the employees will take no time to feel at home. S. This will make the process more interesting and solve the companies' purpose.
questions.explanation: QS is a logical pair. (Q talks about using online games and quizzes and S talks about how this (games and quizzes) can make the transitioning process easier). P cannot be the concluding sentence as it is the one which is introducing the context. Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Identify the correct meaning of the idiom(s) given below. 'Bite the bullet'
options: decide to do something difficult or unpleasant that one has been putting off or hesitating over
a good thing which seemed bad at first
questions.explanation: Bite the bullet means to decide to do something difficult or unpleasant that one has been putting off or hesitating over. Hence, (b) is the right answer.

questions.question: In the following question, four words are given out of which one word is incorrectly spelt. Select the incorrectly spelt word.
questions.explanation: Option C is spelt incorrectly. The correct spelling is 'achievement'.

questions.question: In the following question, four words are given out of which one word is incorrectly spelt. Select the incorrectly spelt word.
questions.explanation: Option B is spelt incorrectly. The correct spelling is 'pinnacle'. It means the most successful point; the culmination.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the words similar in meaning to the word given. ARDENT
questions.explanation: Ardent (adj.) - very enthusiastic or passionate. Avid (adj.) - having or showing a keen interest in or enthusiasm for something. Clearly, 'avid' and 'ardent' have similar meanings; therefore, the answer is option b). Fervour (noun) - intense and passionate feeling; it cannot be the answer as 'ardent' is an adjective and 'fervour' is a noun. Deprecate - disapprove of.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the words similar in meaning to the word given. LAX
questions.explanation: Lax (adj.) - not sufficiently strict, severe, or careful; 'slipshod' conveys a similar meaning; therefore, option b) is the answer. Abated - (of something unpleasant or severe) made less intense or widespread. Stern - strict or severe.

questions.question: In each of the following questions, out of the four alternatives, select the word opposite in meaning to the word given. Abysmal
questions.explanation: Abysmal - Extremely bad; appalling. So, 'superb' can be its antonym. Foolhardy - Recklessly bold or rash. B is the right answer.

questions.question: In each of the following questions, out of the four alternatives, select the word opposite in meaning to the word given. Blight
questions.explanation: Blight - A thing that spoils or damages something. 'Blessing' can be its antonym. A is the right answer. Allude - Suggest or call attention to indirectly; hint at. Florid - Excessively intricate or elaborate.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentence. (Of an investment or investor) not protected against loss by balancing or compensating contracts or transactions.
questions.explanation: Undermine- weaken, spoil, damage. Aid- a source of help or assistance. 'Unhedged' is the correct substitution. Option B is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentence. Involving or showing care and forethought
questions.explanation: Aggravated- made (a problem, injury, or offence) worse or more serious. Brazen- bold; shameless. 'Prudential' can appropriately replace the group of words given in the question. Option D is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the given four alternatives, select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the idiom/phrase. Out of the blue
questions.explanation: If something happens out of the blue, it is completely unexpected. Option C is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, one part of the sentence may or may not have an error. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If the sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'. The actual climate (1)/ impact of the vehicles depends (2)/ heavily on how they're made, what they're replacing, and how long they last. (3)/ No error (4)
questions.explanation: The sentence is free from any error. Option C is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, one part of the sentence may or may not have an error. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If the sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'. Millions of Americans (1)/ are suffering from chronic anxiety, (2)/ the most common mentally illness in the United States. (3)/ No error (4)
questions.explanation: The error is in part 3. An adjective modifies a noun correctly; hence 'mental' (adjective) should replace 'mentally' (adverb) to correctly modify the noun 'illness'. Option D is the correct answer.

questions.question: A
questions.explanation: The sentence tells us that the Finance ministry has initiated a 'process' with PSBs regarding the sharp rise in bad loans. Since the required word is followed by a noun, we need the word to be an adjective. 'Consultative' fits in the context, as the process is between two entities to address a problem. The word in option A is an adverb and that in C is a verb; 'credibility' in option D is a noun. Option B is the right answer. Consultative (adj.) - intended to give professional advice or recommendations; credibility (mass noun) - trustworthiness.

questions.question: B
questions.explanation: The sentence tells us that the Finance ministry has initiated a 'process' with PSBs regarding the sharp rise in bad loans and regarding their role in certain national priorities. Since the required word is preceded by 'to', we need a verb in its root form here (infinitive construction). 'Align' is the one which fits in the context. 'Severe' is an adjective; hence option B is eliminated. Words in options C and D are not in their root form. Option A is the right answer.

questions.question: C
questions.explanation: The sentence tells us that this review process will be the first one after May for the PSBs. 'Direction' is suitable here, to tell us that the process is intended to be sort of a 'roadmap' for the next five years for the PSBs. None of the other options fit in the given context. Option C is the right answer.

questions.question: D
questions.explanation: The sentence tells us that the PSBs have been asked about feedback regarding the rise in NPAs, according to a note. 'Circulated' is the word which can correctly describe the action performed on the 'note'. The words in A and D do not fit in the context. C is incorrect as we need the past participle form of verb after 'had been' (past perfect tense construction). Option B is the right answer.

questions.question: E
questions.explanation: The preceding sentence tells us that the government wants to know the reasons for 'decline' in credit flow. The sentence with the blank gives us the particular parameter the government is interested in - credit to manufacturing. In this context, we can see that 'dropped' is the right word at (E). None of the other words can aptly convey the required meaning here. Option C is the right answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, a sentence has been given in Active/Passive Voice. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Passive/Active Voice. A pay raise is to be received by us.
options: We are receiving a pay raise.
We are to be receiving a pay raise.
We are to receive a pay raise.
We will be receiving a pay raise.
questions.explanation: The rule for changing infinitive from active to passive voice is as follows: "To + root form of the verb" in the active voice is changed to "to + be + past participle" in the passive voice. The reverse will be applicable while changing from passive to active voice. The given sentence is in the passive voice form. Option C is the correct answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions given below, a sentence is given with one blank, followed by 4 options, each having a word which may or may not fit in the blank. From the given options, choose the one that gives the correct word that fits in the blank. The best of social and group constructiveness is an effort to ____________ the psychotic anxieties that lie at the base of every one of us, and which may be provoked under extreme enough conditions.
questions.explanation: The sentence talks about an effort towards 'psychotic anxieties'; the words 'best' and 'constructiveness' indicate that an effort that betters the stated condition should be used; also, the use of 'to' before the blank calls for a verb in its root form. The words 'exacerbate' and 'abduct' do not lend the required meaning to the sentence, moreover 'exacerbate' contradicts the context given above; so, options a) and b) can be eliminated. 'Dispelled' is not a root form of verb; option c) is eliminated too. The word 'allay' (put at rest (fear, suspicion, or worry)), fulfils the conditions mentioned above and hence, option d) is the answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions given below, a sentence is given with one blank, followed by 4 options, each having a word which may or may not fit in the blank. From the given options, choose the one that gives the correct word that fits in the blank. Just building and testing the observatory has proved to be a dauntingly complex technological ___________, pushing the observatory's astronomical price tag to nearly $9 billion and requiring participation from the European and Canadian space agencies.
questions.explanation: The sentence talks about an ongoing work on the observatory and how this all has led to higher expenditure; the blank is preceded by an adjective and hence requires a noun to follow and be qualified by it; so, 'enterprise' which refers to 'a project or undertaking, especially a bold or complex one' rightly fits in the context of the sentence. Since, the sentence talks about a process or a task; also, the sentence labels the noun as 'dauntingly complex'; 'firm' and 'company' are not appropriate in this context; hence, option b) and c) are eliminated. Also, 'undertake' is a verb and does not fit grammatically, thereby eliminating option d). The answer therefore is option a).

questions.question: In each of the questions below, a sentence is given with one or two parts bracketed. Select the option that provides the correct replacement for the bracketed part(s). In case of no improvement needed, choose Option D. The invention of the telescope (made) all minds for (novel) wonders.
questions.explanation: The word 'made' in the sentence does not bring out any meaning. We need a word in the past tense for the first filler. 'Encourage' is in the simple present tense. We need an adjective for the second filler as it describes a noun (wonders). 'Newly' is an adverb. C is the right answer, as 'prepared' and 'new' both fit in the context.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, a sentence is given with one or two parts bracketed. Select the option that provides the correct replacement for the bracketed part(s). In case of no improvement needed, choose Option D. Kepler (owned) a very enthusiastic nature, and was always ready to (hear) to new theories.
questions.explanation: 'Owned' cannot be used for nature, it is used for objects or possessions. Additionally, 'hear' will not be followed by the preposition 'to'. 'Belonged' means to be the property of, which makes no sense. 'Owed' means indebted, which again makes no sense here. Only A fits the context.
